description: Comprehensive PostgreSQL monitoring bundle - three templates and one import file. (1) PostgreSQL Server: database-level monitoring via the PGSQL subagent (BGWriter, Archiver, Replication, GlobalConnections, per-database Stats/Locks/Connections, IsReachable, Version), with 11 thresholds, 4 PG-specific events for instance unreachability, page checksum failures and XID wraparound risk, and 3 object tools (Backends/Locks/Prepared transactions). Auto-binds via Agent.IsSubagentLoaded(PGSQL); two-level instance discovery covers every monitored server and database. (2) PostgreSQL Process Monitoring (Linux): OS-level liveness and resource footprint of the postgres processes on Linux nodes. (3) PostgreSQL Process Monitoring (Windows): the same for postgres.exe on Windows, plus per-process disk I/O. Both process templates auto-bind to nodes whose agent currently sees running PostgreSQL processes and share a PG_SERVICE_DOWN custom event for the count-equals-zero threshold.

events.name: PG_INSTANCE_UNREACHABLE
events.description:
Generated when the PostgreSQL.IsReachable parameter no longer returns YES, indicating the agent cannot connect to the configured PostgreSQL instance.
Parameters carried by the threshold subsystem:
   dciDescription, dciName, currentValue, thresholdValue

events.name: PG_CHECKSUM_FAILURE
events.description:
Generated when PostgreSQL reports new page checksum failures.
Indicates data corruption on disk; investigate hardware and restore from backup if needed.
Parameters carried by the threshold subsystem:
   dciDescription, dciName, currentValue, thresholdValue

events.name: PG_XID_WRAPAROUND_WARNING
events.description:
Generated when PostgreSQL transaction ID consumption is high (oldest XID age > 1.5 billion).
Autovacuum is not freezing tuples fast enough. Database will shut itself down at 2 billion XIDs.
Parameters carried by the threshold subsystem:
   dciDescription, dciName, currentValue, thresholdValue
events.message: PostgreSQL transaction ID consumption high on "%<dciDescription>" (oldest XID age: %<currentValue>)

events.name: PG_XID_WRAPAROUND_IMMINENT
events.description:
Generated when PostgreSQL transaction ID wraparound is imminent (oldest XID age > 1.8 billion).
Database will force single-user mode at 2 billion XIDs and stop accepting connections. URGENT: identify and stop the session blocking autovacuum, or run manual VACUUM FREEZE.
Parameters carried by the threshold subsystem:
   dciDescription, dciName, currentValue, thresholdValue
events.message: PostgreSQL XID wraparound IMMINENT on "%<dciDescription>" (oldest XID age: %<currentValue>) - forced shutdown at 2e9

events.name: PG_SERVICE_DOWN
events.description:
Generated when no PostgreSQL OS process is detected on the host, indicating the database service is down or has crashed.
Parameters carried by the threshold subsystem:
   dciDescription, dciName, currentValue, thresholdValue

templates.name: PostgreSQL Server
templates.comments:
Generic PostgreSQL monitoring template.

Auto-binds to nodes whose agent has the PGSQL subagent loaded with at
least one configured server section. The template uses two-level instance
discovery so a single template covers any number of monitored databases
on any number of monitored servers per agent:

  * Server-wide DCIs (BGWriter, Archiver, Replication, GlobalConnections,
    IsReachable, Version) are discovered against PostgreSQL.DBServers and
    produce one DCI per [PGSQL/servers/<id>] section in pgsql.conf.
  * Per-database DCIs (Stats.*, DBConnections.*, Locks.Total,
    Transactions.Prepared) are discovered against PostgreSQL.AllDatabases
    and produce one DCI per (database, server) pair. Instance values are
    of the form <database>@<server-id>.

==== PostgreSQL user setup (PG 10+) ============================

The login used in pgsql.conf needs LOGIN and the built-in pg_monitor
role:

  -- as superuser:
  CREATE ROLE netxms_monitor WITH LOGIN PASSWORD 'change-me';
  GRANT pg_monitor TO netxms_monitor;

pg_monitor bundles pg_read_all_stats + pg_read_all_settings +
pg_stat_scan_tables. Without it most DCIs return empty or only the
agent's own session's data (Active/Idle counts, OldestXID, Locks.Total,
BGWriter, Archiver, Replication, ChecksumFailures, Backends object
tool's query text). Stats.ChecksumFailures additionally requires PG 12+.

For per-database stats to populate, the agent must be able to read the
size of every measured database. PostgreSQL.AllDatabases reports every
database on the server, but per-DCI values for databases the role cannot
connect to will be empty. PostgreSQL grants CONNECT to PUBLIC by default,
so no explicit grant is normally needed; if PUBLIC connect has been
revoked, grant CONNECT on each measured database:

  GRANT CONNECT ON DATABASE <db> TO netxms_monitor;

==== Agent configuration =======================================

Subagent = pgsql

[PGSQL/servers/<server-id>]
Server   = db_server
Login    = netxms_monitor
Database = postgres            # any DB the user can CONNECT to
Password = change-me

Repeat the [PGSQL/servers/...] section for each PostgreSQL server. The
<server-id> (section suffix) is the value substituted for {instance} in
server-wide DCIs and the @-suffix in per-database DCIs.

==== Excluding databases =======================================

Three layers of filtering are applied to per-database DCIs:

  1. The PostgreSQL agent's SQL excludes template0/template1 and
     any database with no buffer-cache reads since pg_stat_reset().

  2. The instance-discovery filter built into this template drops
     administrative databases of managed PostgreSQL services that
     are never user data: rdsadmin, cloudsqladmin, azure_maintenance,
     azure_sys.

  3. To skip additional databases, set the custom attribute
     Template.PostgreSQL.SkipDatabases on the node (or the template,
     to apply to every bound node) to a comma-separated list of
     regex patterns matched against the database name. Patterns are
     anchored ^...$ so use .* for prefixes/suffixes.

Common deployments find this starter value useful:
  Template.PostgreSQL.SkipDatabases = postgres,repmgr,.*_shadow

Three object tools are shipped for live diagnostics: PostgreSQL Backends,
Locks, and Prepared transactions. They run on demand and prompt for the
server-id.

templates.name: PostgreSQL Process Monitoring (Linux)
templates.comments:
OS-level monitoring of PostgreSQL processes on Linux nodes.

Auto-binds to Linux nodes whose agent currently sees at least one
process named 'postgres'. Tracks aggregate (sum across all postgres
processes - postmaster, every backend, every background worker)
memory footprint, CPU time, threads, page faults and open file
descriptors. Designed to complement the database-level template
(PostgreSQL Server) with a service-liveness signal that does not
depend on the PGSQL subagent or database connectivity.

==== Service-down detection ===================================

The Process.Count(postgres) DCI carries a threshold that fires the
PG_SERVICE_DOWN event when the count drops to zero for two
consecutive samples. The two-sample requirement avoids false
positives during a graceful restart.

==== Memory threshold =========================================

Process.MemoryUsage(postgres,sum) measures the combined PostgreSQL
RAM share of the host's total memory and warns above 80%% averaged
over three samples - a value that high typically indicates either
shared_buffers misconfiguration or runaway connection counts.

==== Adjusting the memory threshold ===========================

The Process.MemoryUsage threshold defaults to 80%% (averaged over
three samples). Set the custom attribute Threshold.PostgreSQLProcessHighMemoryUsage on the node (or on the
template, to apply to every bound node) to override the percentage
without editing the template. Both the Linux and Windows process
templates read the same attribute name, so a single value adjusts
both. Using the same mechanism as the disk-usage thresholds in the
stock Linux/Windows templates (Threshold.FileSystemLowFreeSpace,
etc.).

dcis.name: Process.Count(postgres)
dcis.description: PostgreSQL processes: number of running postgres processes

dcis.name: Process.MemoryUsage(postgres,sum)
dcis.description: PostgreSQL processes: memory usage as percentage of system RAM

dcis.name: Process.RSS(postgres,sum)
dcis.description: PostgreSQL processes: total resident set size (working set)

dcis.name: Process.VMSize(postgres,sum)
dcis.description: PostgreSQL processes: total virtual memory size

dcis.name: Process.CPUTime(postgres,sum)
dcis.description: PostgreSQL processes: CPU time consumed per minute

dcis.name: Process.Threads(postgres,sum)
dcis.description: PostgreSQL processes: total number of threads across all processes

dcis.name: Process.PageFaults(postgres,sum)
dcis.description: PostgreSQL processes: page faults per minute

dcis.name: Process.Handles(postgres,sum)
dcis.description: PostgreSQL processes: total open handles / file descriptors

templates.name: PostgreSQL Process Monitoring (Windows)
templates.comments:
OS-level monitoring of PostgreSQL processes on Windows nodes.

Auto-binds to Windows nodes whose agent currently sees at least one
process named 'postgres.exe'. Tracks aggregate (sum across every
postgres.exe process - postmaster, every backend, every background
worker) memory footprint, CPU time, threads, page faults, open
handles and disk I/O. Designed to complement the database-level
template (PostgreSQL Server) with a service-liveness signal that
does not depend on the PGSQL subagent or database connectivity.

==== Service-down detection ===================================

The Process.Count(postgres.exe) DCI carries a threshold that fires
the PG_SERVICE_DOWN event when the count drops to zero for two
consecutive samples. Two samples avoid false positives during a
graceful service restart.

==== Memory threshold =========================================

Process.MemoryUsage(postgres.exe,sum) measures the combined
PostgreSQL RAM share of the host's total memory and warns above
80%% averaged over three samples.

==== Disk I/O =================================================

Process.IO.ReadB / WriteB give per-minute disk I/O byte rates.
These are Windows-only metrics and are not present in the Linux
process template - the equivalent Linux signal is on the host as
a whole (System.IO.*) since Linux does not expose per-process
block-device I/O via the standard Process.* family.

==== Adjusting the memory threshold ===========================

The Process.MemoryUsage threshold defaults to 80%% (averaged over
three samples). Set the custom attribute Threshold.PostgreSQLProcessHighMemoryUsage on the node (or on the
template, to apply to every bound node) to override the percentage
without editing the template. Both the Linux and Windows process
templates read the same attribute name, so a single value adjusts
both. Using the same mechanism as the disk-usage thresholds in the
stock Linux/Windows templates (Threshold.FileSystemLowFreeSpace,
etc.).

dcis.name: Process.Count(postgres.exe)
dcis.description: PostgreSQL processes: number of running postgres.exe processes

dcis.name: Process.MemoryUsage(postgres.exe,sum)
dcis.description: PostgreSQL processes: memory usage as percentage of system RAM

dcis.name: Process.RSS(postgres.exe,sum)
dcis.description: PostgreSQL processes: total resident set size (working set)

dcis.name: Process.VMSize(postgres.exe,sum)
dcis.description: PostgreSQL processes: total virtual memory size

dcis.name: Process.CPUTime(postgres.exe,sum)
dcis.description: PostgreSQL processes: CPU time consumed per minute

dcis.name: Process.Threads(postgres.exe,sum)
dcis.description: PostgreSQL processes: total number of threads across all processes

dcis.name: Process.PageFaults(postgres.exe,sum)
dcis.description: PostgreSQL processes: page faults per minute

dcis.name: Process.Handles(postgres.exe,sum)
dcis.description: PostgreSQL processes: total open handles / file descriptors

dcis.name: Process.IO.ReadB(postgres.exe,sum)
dcis.description: PostgreSQL processes: bytes read from disk per minute

dcis.name: Process.IO.WriteB(postgres.exe,sum)
dcis.description: PostgreSQL processes: bytes written to disk per minute

rules.comments: Generate alarms for PostgreSQL integrity, XID-wraparound, and process-level service-down events. Alarm severity follows the event severity so actions and notifications see the same level. Alarm key matches the system threshold rule so SYS_THRESHOLD_REARMED auto-terminates.
